question: Compare the design features in Table 1.2-2 with the material specifications in Table 5.2-4 to show how design choices enhance safety.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It explains the types of interface requirements (conceptual design information and COL information items), and specifies that the COL applicant must address site-specific information and any departures from the certified design. Tables referenced in the section summarize these interfaces and list the required COL information items.

nuscale_ch01_0118.title: 1.8 Interfaces with Certified Design
nuscale_ch01_0118.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It describes the types of interface requirements (conceptual design information and COL information items), identifies the boundaries and responsibilities for each, and notes that the COL applicant must address any departures from the certified design and provide required site-specific information.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with NRC regulations and ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) requirements for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details the use of specific ASME code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing, ensuring all selected code cases meet NRC-endorsed conditions as specified in relevant regulatory guides. Table 5.2-1 consolidates the specific code cases applied.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0351.title: SRP 9.3.2, Rev 3: Process and Post-Accident Sampling SystemsII.2 Technical Specifications
nuscale_ch01_0351.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for process and post-accident sampling systems, noting partial or full compliance depending on system applicability and design differences. Specifically, technical specifications for sampling systems are deemed not applicable due to prior NRC approval, while other aspects such as functional design, seismic classification, and environmental protection generally conform to requirements. For the Chemical and Volume Control System, only safety-related functions preventing inadvertent boron dilution are addressed, with single-failure criteria applied to relevant isolation valves.

nuscale_ch01_0133.title: 8.39 Release of Patients Administered Radioactive Materials
nuscale_ch01_0133.summary: Section 8.39 states that the guidance on the release of patients administered radioactive materials is not applicable to the NuScale facility, as it only applies to facilities that use radio-pharmaceuticals. Therefore, the requirements in this section do not pertain to NuScale's operations.

nuscale_ch01_0519.title: DSRS 11.6, Rev 0: Guidance on Instrumentation and Control Design Features for Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring, and Area Radiation and Airborne Radioactivity MonitoringII.9 Description of design features and instrumentation used in primary and secondary coolant system leakage detection
nuscale_ch01_0519.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with DSRS 11.6, Rev 0, specifically regarding the design features and instrumentation used for detecting leakage in primary and secondary coolant systems. It states that the design conforms to regulatory guidance, with references to relevant sections of the safety analysis report, and notes that no exceptions or additional requirements are identified for this criterion.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure boundary to prevent the release of fission products and provides structural support for various reactor components. Materials used for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch01_0514.title: DSRS 11.6, Rev 0: Guidance on Instrumentation and Control Design Features for Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring, and Area Radiation and Airborne Radioactivity MonitoringII.4 Compliance with GDC 63 & 64 via post-TMI action plan items
nuscale_ch01_0514.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for instrumentation and control design features related to radiological monitoring, specifically addressing compliance with GDC 63 and 64 through post-TMI action plan items. It notes that while most requirements are met, certain aspects related to testing and operational programs fall under the responsibility of the COL applicant and are only partially addressed in this submission.

nuscale_ch01_0356.title: SRP 9.3.3, Rev 3: Equipment and Floor Drainage SystemII.3 Control of Releases of Radioactive Material to the Environment
nuscale_ch01_0356.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale equipment and floor drainage system meets regulatory criteria for controlling releases of radioactive material to the environment, with no parts of the drain system penetrating the containment barrier. It also notes that the system conforms to requirements for protection against natural phenomena and environmental effects.

nuscale_ch01_0557.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.8 RG 8.10
nuscale_ch01_0557.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4. It details whether each regulatory guide is fully conformed to, partially conformed to, or not applicable, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Specifically, RG 8.10 is noted as not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each guide is applicable, and if so, whether NuScale’s design fully or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, specifying that key components are constructed from low alloy steel, austenitic stainless steel, and nickel-based alloys, all in accordance with relevant BPVC Section II specifications. It also notes the use of corrosion-resistant cladding and materials compatible with the operating environment for various pressurizer parts.

edges.description: Section A outlines the design features and boundaries of certified systems, while Section B details the material specifications and code compliance for those systems, showing that the design choices in A are implemented using the material standards in B to enhance safety.

Section A identifies the structures, systems, and components included in the certified design, which are further detailed in Section B through references to material specifications and standards that ensure the integrity and safety of those components.

Section A identifies the certified design features and their boundaries, while Section B specifies the material codes and standards applied to those features, connecting design choices to safety through compliance with regulatory material specifications.

Section A identifies the certified design features and their boundaries, while Section B lists references (including material standards) that inform the material specifications for those features, connecting design choices to safety-related material requirements.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME BPVC code cases for design and fabrication, while Section B lists references including material reliability and quality assurance standards that inform or support those code cases, connecting design features to material specifications for safety enhancement.

Section A discusses the technical specifications and regulatory conformance of the sampling systems, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and identifies which structures, systems, and components (including those in Table 1.2-2) are part of the certified design, thereby connecting the regulatory requirements in A to the design features in B.

Section A references regulatory acceptance criteria and their applicability to the NuScale design, while Section B specifies the interface requirements and identifies the certified design boundaries, connecting them by clarifying which design features and material specifications are governed by regulatory criteria.

Section A references the general regulatory acceptance criteria for sampling system technical specifications, while Section B specifies the material codes and standards (ASME BPVC) used in the design and construction of safety-related systems, connecting the regulatory requirements to the concrete material choices that enhance safety.

Section A discusses regulatory guidance that is not applicable to the NuScale design, while Section B outlines the interface requirements between the certified design and site-specific design, connecting them through the scope of regulatory applicability and responsibility for compliance.

Section A discusses regulatory guidance that is not applicable to the NuScale design, while Section B outlines the interface requirements between the certified design and site-specific design, with both sections referencing the scope and applicability of regulatory requirements to different aspects of the facility.

Section A provides regulatory guidance and design feature requirements for radiological monitoring systems, while Section B identifies the certified design boundaries and interface requirements, connecting them by specifying which systems (from Section A) are included in the certified design scope (from Section B).

Section A outlines the regulatory guidance and design features for radiological monitoring instrumentation, while Section B defines the interface requirements and identifies which structures, systems, and components (including those in Table 1.2-2) are part of the certified design, thus connecting the specific design features to their regulatory context and boundaries.

Section A outlines the design features and instrumentation requirements for radiological monitoring, while Section B details the material specifications and code compliance for overpressure protection, with both sections connected through the way material choices (Section B) support the implementation and effectiveness of the safety-related design features described in Section A.

Section A describes the reactor vessel's design features as part of the certified design, while Section B outlines how these features (identified in Section 1.2 and Table 1.2-2) interface with site-specific requirements, connecting the vessel's design to broader safety and regulatory boundaries.

Section A describes the reactor vessel's design and its role as a pressure-retaining component, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and identifies the reactor vessel as part of the certified design, connecting them through the inclusion and specification of the reactor vessel within the certified design boundaries.

Section A describes the reactor vessel's design as a pressure-retaining component, while Section B outlines the regulatory and code compliance requirements (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the reactor vessel's materials and construction must satisfy, directly connecting the vessel's design features to the mandated material specifications for safety.

Section A outlines the regulatory guidance and acceptance criteria for instrumentation and control design features, while Section B specifies the interface requirements and identifies which structures, systems, and components (including those in Table 1.2-2) are part of the certified design, thus connecting the regulatory expectations to the actual design features that must be addressed for safety.

Section A outlines the regulatory guidance and acceptance criteria for instrumentation and control design features, while Section B specifies the interface requirements and boundaries for certified and site-specific design elements, connecting them through the need to ensure that design features (from Table 1.2-2) and material specifications (from Table 5.2-4) are compatible and compliant within the certified design framework.

The design features listed in Table 1.2-2 (Section A) are implemented using the material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4 (Section B), demonstrating that the chosen materials directly support the safety-enhancing design choices.

Section A discusses regulatory criteria and conformance for specific systems in the NuScale design, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and identifies which structures, systems, and components are included in the certified design, thereby connecting the regulatory expectations in A to the design scope defined in B.

Section A discusses regulatory criteria and conformance for equipment and drainage systems, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between certified and site-specific design, connecting them through the identification of which systems (from Section A) are included in the certified design as referenced in Section B.

Section A discusses regulatory criteria and acceptance for equipment and drainage systems, while Section B details compliance with ASME code cases for material specifications, connecting them through the requirement that design features (Section A) must use materials and construction methods (Section B) that meet regulatory safety standards.

Section A references the applicability and conformance of sampling system technical specifications, while Section B describes the design features and instrumentation for radiological monitoring, both connecting through their focus on how design and material choices ensure representative sampling and enhance safety.

Section A outlines the regulatory acceptance criteria and applicability for process and post-accident sampling systems, while Section B describes the physical design and material composition of the reactor vessel, connecting them through the need for the vessel's material specifications (from Table 5.2-4) to satisfy the safety-related design features (from Table 1.2-2) required by regulatory criteria in Section A.

Section A references the technical specifications and sampling system requirements for safety analysis, while Section B specifies the instrumentation and design features for radiological monitoring, with both sections connected through their focus on design features and specifications that ensure compliance with regulatory safety criteria.

Section A and Section B both reference the conformance of the NuScale design with SRP 9.3.2 requirements, specifically noting partial applicability and exceptions due to design differences, thus connecting them through their evaluation of regulatory compliance for related systems.

Section A discusses regulatory guidance that is not applicable to the facility, while Section B outlines regulatory criteria for radiological monitoring instrumentation, with both sections referencing the applicability of regulatory requirements to the NuScale design, thus connecting through their treatment of regulatory scope and responsibility.

Section A provides regulatory guidance and requirements for instrumentation and control design features related to radiological monitoring, which are directly supported by the material specifications and structural design features of the reactor vessel described in Section B, ensuring that the chosen materials and construction methods enhance the safety and effectiveness of leakage detection and radiological monitoring systems.

Section A describes specific design features and instrumentation for coolant system leakage detection, while Section B outlines the regulatory compliance requirements (GDC 63 & 64) that these design features must satisfy, connecting the technical implementation to the regulatory safety objectives.

The design features listed in Table 1.2-2 (Section A) are implemented using materials specified in Table 5.2-4 (Section B), and the selection of these materials directly supports the safety enhancements intended by the design choices.

Section A describes the physical design and components of the reactor vessel, while Section B outlines regulatory guidance and criteria for instrumentation and control features that must be met by those design choices, connecting the vessel's design to compliance with safety monitoring requirements.

Section A describes the reactor vessel's design features, while Section B outlines regulatory criteria for equipment and drainage systems, so the relationship is that Section A's design features are evaluated for their ability to satisfy the safety requirements specified in Section B.

Section A describes the design features and instrumentation for radiological monitoring, which are implemented using the material specifications outlined in Section B to ensure that the design choices effectively control radioactive releases and enhance safety.

Section A outlines radiation protection design features and their regulatory conformance, while Section B details the interface requirements and boundaries between certified and site-specific designs, connecting them through the identification and specification of structures, systems, and components that must meet the design features described in Section A.

Section A outlines radiation protection design features and their regulatory conformance, while Section B defines the interface requirements and identifies the structures, systems, and components included in the certified design, thereby connecting the specific design features in Table 1.2-2 (referenced in the analysis question) to the broader certified design context.

The design features listed in Table 1.2-2 (Section A) are implemented using the material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4 (Section B), demonstrating that the chosen materials directly support and enhance the safety functions of the design features.

Section A lists radiation protection design features and their regulatory conformance, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between certified and site-specific design, connecting them through the identification and allocation of safety-related structures, systems, and components that must meet both design and material specifications for enhanced safety.

Section A outlines radiation protection design features and their regulatory conformance, while Section B defines the interface requirements and boundaries for certified and site-specific design elements, connecting them by specifying which design features (from Section A) are included in the certified design scope (from Section B).

Section A outlines radiation protection design features and their regulatory conformance, while Section B details material specifications and code compliance for overpressure protection, with both sections connected through the use of design choices (from A) that are implemented using specific material specifications (from B) to enhance overall safety.

Section A describes the design features that maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B identifies the structures, systems, and components included in the certified design, thus Section B provides the general scope within which Section A's specific design features are implemented.

Section A describes the design features that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B outlines the certified design interfaces and identifies the structures, systems, and components (referenced in Table 1.2-2) that must meet specific material specifications (such as those in Table 5.2-4), thus connecting design choices to material requirements for safety.

Section A describes the design features intended to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B specifies the material codes and regulatory compliance (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that those design features must adhere to, directly linking design choices to material specifications for safety.

Section A specifies the materials used for pressurizer components, referencing Section 5.2 for details, while Section B identifies which structures, systems, and components (including those in Table 1.2-2) are part of the certified design, thus connecting the material choices in A to the design features listed in B.

Section A specifies the materials used for key pressurizer components, referencing Section 5.2 for detailed material specifications, while Section B identifies which structures, systems, and components (including the pressurizer) are part of the certified design, thus connecting the design features in Table 1.2-2 to the material specifications in Table 5.2-4 by defining their scope and boundaries.

Section A describes the specific materials used in pressurizer components, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that these material specifications must comply with to ensure reactor coolant boundary integrity and overpressure protection.

Section A and Section B both list the conformance of radiation protection design features with regulatory guides, providing the regulatory context that connects the design features in Table 1.2-2 with the material specifications in Table 5.2-4, thereby supporting the analysis of how design choices (from Table 1.2-2) are justified or enhanced by material specifications (from Table 5.2-4) to meet safety requirements.

The design features listed in Table 1.2-2 (Section A) are implemented using the material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4 (Section B), demonstrating that the selection of appropriate materials directly supports the safety-enhancing design choices.

Section A outlines radiation protection design features and their regulatory conformance, while Section B details the material specifications for pressurizer components, with the connection being that the material choices in Section B directly support the safety objectives described in Section A by enhancing radiation protection.

The design features listed in Table 1.2-2 (Section A) are implemented using the material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4 (Section B), demonstrating that the selected materials directly support the safety-enhancing design choices.

Section A outlines radiation protection design features and their regulatory conformance, while Section B details the material specifications for reactor coolant system components, with the connection being that the material choices in Section B directly support the safety and radiation protection objectives described in Section A.

Section A outlines the design features intended to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B specifies the material choices for key components (such as the pressurizer and RPV upper shell) that directly support these design features, showing how material selection enhances boundary integrity and thus safety.
